description: Fix inaccuracies in MCP server reference and architecture documentation: health endpoint response shapes, wire protocol methods, and rate limiting claims
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix health endpoint response shape in MCP server reference
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want accurate /health endpoint documentation so that I can correctly parse the response in integrations
acceptanceCriteria: mcp-server.mdx shows lastReloadTimestamp as a number (milliseconds since epoch), not an ISO 8601 string
mcp-server.mdx shows fileWatcher.watchedPlugins as a number (count), not a string array
mcp-server.mdx shows fileWatcher.pendingPlugins as a number (count), not a string array
mcp-server.mdx shows fileWatcher.lastPollAt as a number or null (ms since epoch), not an ISO string
The example JSON response block is updated to reflect correct types
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
In docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx, the /health endpoint example response (lines 142-185) has incorrect types:

1. Line 168: `"lastReloadTimestamp": "2026-02-21T10:15:00.000Z"` — should be a number like `1708512900000`. See platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts line 309: `lastReloadTimestamp: hs?.lastReloadTimestamp ?? 0` where lastReloadTimestamp is Date.now().

2. Lines 173-175: `"watchedPlugins": ["slack"]`, `"pendingPlugins": []` — should be numbers. See http-routes.ts lines 282-283: `watchedPlugins` is `state.fileWatching.entries.length - pendingPlugins` (a number), `pendingPlugins` is also a number.

3. Line 175: `"lastPollAt": "2026-02-21T10:30:00.000Z"` — should be a number or null. See http-routes.ts line 315: `lastPollAt: state.fileWatching.mtimeLastPollAt` (number | null).

Also update the field descriptions table below the example to match. Also, line 201 says fileWatcher is 'dev mode only' but it's always included in authenticated responses (values are just 0/null in production mode).

userStories.title: Fix /ws-info documentation and rate limiting claims
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want accurate /ws-info endpoint documentation so that I understand the response shape and auth requirements
acceptanceCriteria: mcp-server.mdx /ws-info response example includes the wsSecret field (conditionally present when a secret is configured)
mcp-server.mdx does NOT claim /ws-info is rate-limited (only /reload and /extension/reload are rate-limited)
architecture.mdx does NOT claim /ws-info or /audit are rate-limited
The rate-limiting section in mcp-server.mdx correctly lists only /reload and /extension/reload as rate-limited endpoints
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
Multiple related issues about /ws-info and rate limiting:

1. docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx lines 218-221: Response example shows only `{ "wsUrl": "ws://localhost:9515/ws" }` but the actual response also includes `wsSecret` when a secret is configured. See platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts line 243: `{ wsUrl, ...(state.wsSecret ? { wsSecret: state.wsSecret } : {}) }`.

2. Rate limiting: Only /reload and /extension/reload call checkEndpointRateLimit() in http-routes.ts (lines 352, 373). Fix:
   - mcp-server.mdx line 38 table row: remove rate-limiting claim from /ws-info
   - mcp-server.mdx lines 207-208: remove 'rate-limited to 10 requests/minute'
   - mcp-server.mdx lines 349-351: update rate-limiting section to list only /reload and /extension/reload
   - architecture.mdx line 367: fix 'Admin endpoints (/reload, /extension/reload, /ws-info, /audit): Bearer token required, plus rate limiting' — remove /ws-info and /audit from rate-limited list

userStories.title: Add missing wire protocol methods and fix discovery pipeline description
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want complete wire protocol documentation so that I can understand all Extension-to-Server communication
acceptanceCriteria: mcp-server.mdx Extension -> Server wire protocol table includes plugin.search, plugin.install, plugin.updateFromRegistry, plugin.remove, and plugin.checkUpdates methods
architecture.mdx Extension -> Server wire protocol table includes the same five missing methods
architecture.mdx describes the discovery pipeline as five phases (discover npm, resolve local, load in parallel, merge, build registry) instead of four
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
Two issues:

1. Wire protocol: platform/mcp-server/src/extension-protocol.ts lines 404-427 handles five Extension->Server methods not in docs: `plugin.search`, `plugin.install`, `plugin.updateFromRegistry`, `plugin.remove`, `plugin.checkUpdates`. Add these to the wire protocol table in:
   - docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx (lines 430-488, Extension -> Server table)
   - docs/content/docs/contributing/architecture.mdx (lines 276-323, Extension -> Server table)
   For each method, read the handler in extension-protocol.ts to determine the params and return shape.

2. Discovery pipeline: docs/content/docs/contributing/architecture.mdx lines 76-83 says four phases. The actual code in platform/mcp-server/src/discovery.ts (lines 37-44) describes five phases: 1) Discover npm plugins (auto-discovery), 2) Resolve local plugin paths, 3) Load all plugins in parallel, 4) Merge (local overrides npm), 5) Build immutable registry. Update architecture.mdx to describe five phases.
